free_text: Mass, charge, and energy distributions in very
asymmetric thermal fission of 235-U

running in 'delta E - E' mode.

J.L.S., M.B.

J.P.B., H.R.F. And experimental site.
R.B.
Grenoble high-flux reactor.
Thermal reactor spectrum.
The ILL recoil spectrometer Lohengrin
The fission products are selected in flight
according to the magnetic and electric field settings,
was used for the measurements.
An ionization chamber running in 'delta e - e'
mode was used to detect the fission products. Energy
resolution of the chamber allowed separation of
neighbouring a/q values (mass over ionic charge
ratios) differing in energy by about 5 MeV. Mass and
atomic number of the isotopes were determined
unambiguously, the entrance slit of the chamber
selected a 1 MeV energy window.
The sample consisted of a 40 ug/cm2 layer of UO2,
enriched to 99.5% in 235-U, on a titanium
backing and covered with a Ni layer of 250 ug/cm2
thickness. The burn-up was less than 5%/day.
The fragment energies were corrected for the losses in
the sample material (approx. 0.7 MeV) and the covering
Ni layer (approx. 5 MeV). The data were corrected for
energy dispersion of the spectrometer and for the
burnup of the sample.
The uncertainties due to the corrections and
summations were included in the yield errors.
Data obtained from J.L.Sida.
S.W.
Date is corrected. BIB information added.

.The nuclear charge yields were obtained for a given
mass, energy and ionic charge state by an analysis of
the 'delta e' spectrum from the ionization chamber.
The tabulated yields are then produced by integrating
over the relevant ionic charge states.
